name: Le cap du Carmil – trail starting from Col des Marrous – Parc naturel régional des Pyrénées Ariégeoises
name_original: Le cap du Carmil – trail au départ du Col des Marrous – Parc naturel régional des Pyrénées Ariégeoises
region_intro: Jogging around Alzen offers a variety of routes through diverse natural settings. The region features trails that traverse natural parks, including paths with significant elevation changes and routes around lakes. Runners can expect a mix of terrain, from established greenways to more challenging mountain trails. Best jogging routes around Alzen The most popular jogging route is Le cap du Carmil – trail starting from Col des Marrous – Parc naturel régional des Pyrénées Ariégeoises, a 8.4 miles (13.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 58 minutes to complete. This difficult route features over 600 meters of elevation gain through a natural park. Another top favourite among local runners is Running loop from Cadarcet, a moderate 6.7 miles (10.8 km) path. This route offers a scenic loop with gentle elevation changes, ideal for a steady run. Local runners also love the Lac de Mondély loop from Montels, a 12.4 miles (20.0 km) trail leading through varied terrain around a lake, often completed in about 2 hours 27 minutes. Jogging around Alzen is defined by natural park trails, lake loops, and greenways, offering a range of experiences from flat paths to routes with considerable climbs. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more difficult mountain trails. The routes in Alzen are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 25 reviews. More than 1000 runners have used komoot to explore Alzen's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Alzen?
A: There are over 70 running routes available around Alzen, offering a wide range of experiences for different fitness levels. These include paths through natural parks, around lakes, and along greenways.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on jogging trails near Alzen?
A: The jogging trails around Alzen feature a diverse mix of terrain. You'll find everything from established greenways with gentle elevation changes to more challenging mountain trails with significant climbs, particularly within the natural parks.

Q: Are there any easy jogging routes for beginners in Alzen?
A: Yes, there are a few easy routes suitable for beginners. While many trails are moderate to difficult, you can find options that offer a gentler experience. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/41542576" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Greenway of Montels loop from Cadarcet&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route that provides a scenic run without extreme elevation.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Alzen?
A: The running routes in Alzen are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 25 reviews. Runners often praise the diverse natural settings, from serene lake loops to challenging mountain trails, and the variety of experiences available.

Q: Are there any circular running routes around Alzen?
A: Yes, several circular running routes are available. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/35369943" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Cadarcet&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 6.7-mile path offering a scenic loop with gentle elevation changes. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24684595" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lac de Mondély loop from Montels&lt;/a&gt;, a longer 12.4-mile trail around a lake.

Q: Can I find running routes with significant elevation changes for a more challenging workout?
A: Absolutely. Alzen offers several challenging routes with considerable elevation gains. For instance, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974172309/le-cap-du-carmil-trail-starting-from-col-des-marrous-parc-naturel-regional-des-pyrenees-ariegeoises" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Le cap du Carmil – trail starting from Col des Marrous – Parc naturel régional des Pyrénées Ariégeoises&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 8.4-mile trail featuring over 600 meters of elevation gain through a natural park.

Q: Are there any interesting landmarks or attractions near the running trails in Alzen?
A: While Alzen itself is not widely recognized as a geographical location for outdoor activities, the routes listed are situated in areas with notable features. You might encounter greenways like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2588853" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vernajoul Viaduct Greenway&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5152815" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Foix–Saint-Girons Greenway&lt;/a&gt;. There are also mountain passes such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/188107" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Col de la Crouzette (1245 m)&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/122431" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Péguère Pass&lt;/a&gt;, which offer scenic views.

Q: What is the best time of year for jogging in this region?
A: Given the presence of natural parks and varied terrain, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for jogging, with milder temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summer can be warm, especially on exposed trails, while winter might present challenges on higher elevation routes.

Q: Are there any running routes that pass by lakes?
A: Yes, you can find running routes that incorporate lakes. A notable example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24684595" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lac de Mondély loop from Montels&lt;/a&gt;, which is a 12.4-mile trail leading through varied terrain around a lake, offering picturesque views during your run.

Q: How long do the running routes typically take to complete?
A: The duration of running routes varies significantly based on distance and difficulty. Shorter, moderate loops like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/35369943" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Cadarcet&lt;/a&gt; can take around 1 hour 8 minutes, while longer, more difficult trails such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974172309/le-cap-du-carmil-trail-starting-from-col-des-marrous-parc-naturel-regional-des-pyrenees-ariegeoises" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Le cap du Carmil&lt;/a&gt; might take closer to 2 hours.

Q: Are there any greenway trails suitable for running?
A: Yes, the region features greenways that are excellent for running. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/41542576" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Greenway of Montels loop from Cadarcet&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 4.1-mile route that utilizes a greenway, providing a smooth and enjoyable surface for your run.
